regions.text: Thinking is complex self-control. Our brains use feedback systems where effects control their causes.
Thermostat loop: Fuel produces heat, which feeds back through the thermostat to limit more fuel. (Diagram: Feedback circle for a thermostat).
Flyball governor loop: Steam produces work, which feeds back to limit more steam if the engine is too fast. (Diagram: Feedback circle for a governor).
The human brain contains millions of such feedback circles constantly controlling each other.
